div.head: The Minister in China (Reinsch) to the Acting Secretary of State

opener.dateline:
Peking, May 28, 1919, 8 p.m.
[Received May 29, 8.15 a.m.]Text printed from corrected copy received May 30, 3.40 p.m.

div.p:
Your May 21st [22d], 2 p.m. Arnold states that delegates Tariff Revision Commission did not sign draft revised tariff, as some feared they would thereby prematurely commit their Governments. Printed draft and printed appendix, as forwarded Arnold’s despatch of
April 3rd, to the Department, authentic. Foreign Office will cable Chinese Legation authorizing acceptance printed draft forwarded by Arnold as the basis for convention and empowering Chargé d’Affaires to sign.
